title: Drug Administration Food Interactions 2025 Care Givers Professionals EDITION

data: Taken with meals to decrease GIT upset.

For patients who have gastrointestinal intolerance of Metformin, slower titration, ensuring that the patient is taking the medication with food, or switching to an extended-release formulation may improve tolerability.
Patients are advised to take supplemental Vitamin B12 (Calcium also decrease metformin-induced Vitamin B12 deficiency).

Short+ Intermediate (mixtard 30, humulin 70/30), Rapid + Intermediate (Novomix, Humalog mix), Rapid + Long (Ryzodeg), GLP-1 agonist + Long (Xultophy, SOLIQUA)
Short+ Intermediate: 2 divided doses daily 30–45 min before meal.
Rapid + Intermediate: 2 divided doses daily within 15 min before meal.
Novomix: When necessary may be used immediately after meals.
Rapid + Long (Ryzodeg): Once or twice per day, with any main meal.
Xultophy: Once with or without food at same time daily.
SOLIQUA: Once within one hour before first meal.

Administer during or up to 1 hour after each main meal containing fat.
Separate dose by at least 2 hours from multivitamin containing fat-soluble vitamins (A, D, E, K).
Skip dose if a meal is missed or contains no fat.
GI side effects of orlistat may increase if taken with any one meal very high in fat or diet with > 30% fat.

Typically taken once daily in the morning to avoid night time urination (nocturia).
Take the last dose of multiple doses no later than 6 PM unless instructed otherwise.

In the evening (especially short-acting statins like simvastatin).
Long-acting ones (atorvastatin, rosuvastatin) can be taken any time.

Avoid grapefruit and its juice, as it can increase statin levels and cause side effects.

Warfarin's effectiveness can be affected by Vitamin K intake, as it works by antagonizing this vitamin.
Consistency in Dietary Vitamin K intake (Green Leafy Vegetables: Kale, spinach, broccoli, collards, turnip greens, Brussels sprouts, Green tea) is critical to maintaining stable blood thinning.
Cranberry and Grapefruit Juice: Both may inhibit warfarin metabolism, increasing bleeding risk.

Administer after meals with plenty of fluids (fluids sufficient to yield daily urinary output of at least 2 Liter and to maintain a neutral or slightly alkaline urine).

Administer in the morning and ≥30 minutes before the first food, beverage (except plain water), or other medication(s) of the day.
Patients should stay upright (not to lie down) for ≥30 minutes and until after first food of the day (to reduce esophageal irritation).
The tablet should be swallowed whole; do not chew or suck.
Co-administration with dairy products or calcium-rich foods (e.g., milk, cheese, yogurt) may decrease alendronate absorption.
Don't take with mineral water or with other beverages.
Beverages (especially orange juice, coffee, and mineral water) and food may reduce the absorption of alendronate as much as 60%.

Administer consistently in the morning on an empty stomach, at least 30 to 60 minutes before breakfast or at bedtime (3-4 hours after dinner).
Alternatively, may administer at night 3 to 4 hours after the meal (e.g.: in Ramadan).

Do not administer within 4 hours of Calcium-rich foods (e.g., milk, yogurt, cheese), Iron-rich foods (e.g., red meat, lentils, spinach), and High-fiber foods (e.g., whole grains, broccoli), Soy products (e.g., tofu, soy milk) and Bile acid sequestrants (Cholestyramine).

Administer consistently at the same time every day with food (≥350 calories).
Evening administration may help reduce adverse effects.
Administration with food (≥350 calories) increased Cmax and AUC of Lurasidone ~3 times and 2 times, respectively, compared to administration under fasting conditions.
Exposure was not affected by the fat content of the meal.

Phenytoin may decrease mucosal uptake of folic acid and can lead to folic acid deficiency and megaloblastic anaemia.
Phenytoin use has been associated with low serum concentrations of Vitamin B2 (riboflavin), Vitamin B6 (pyridoxine) and Vitamin B12 (cyanocobalamin).
Phenytoin interferes with Vitamin D Metabolism and leads to Osteomalacia and Hypocalcemia, so Vitamin D supplements may be needed.

Generally PPIs should be taken in the morning on empty stomach: Morning PPI: 30-60 minutes before breakfast & Antacids, minimum 2 hours separation.
Evening PPI (if prescribed): 30-60 minutes before dinner.
Omeprazole/Esomeprazole: Take on empty stomach.
Nexium sachets (powder form) can mix with apple juice (Avoid grapefruit juice).
Dexlansoprazole: Can be taken with food.
May open the capsule, sprinkle the intact granules onto 1 tablespoon of applesauce, and swallow intact granules immediately (do not chew granules).
Do not save applesauce and granule mixture for later use.
Avoid within 2 hours: Antacids, Iron & Calcium supplements, Sucralfate.
Prolonged treatment (more than 1-2 years) may lead to malabsorption of dietary Vitamin B12, Calcium and Magnesium.

Swallow whole capsule; don't crush or chew.

Maintain adequate hydration during therapy (to minimize nephrolithiasis risk).

High-fat meals decrease the bioavailability of fexofenadine by ~50%.
Fruit juice (apple, grapefruit, orange) may decrease bioavailability of fexofenadine by ~36%.
So separating consumption by at least 4 hours may decrease the significance of this interaction.

1 hour before or 2 hours after intake of food or fruit juices.

May administer without regard to food or meals.
When treating asthma, administer dose in the evening.
Patients with allergic rhinitis may individualize administration time (morning or evening).
Patients with both asthma and allergic rhinitis should take their dose in the evening.

Granules: May be administered directly into the mouth, dissolved in 5 mL of cold or room temperature baby formula or breast milk, or mixed with a spoonful of cold or room temperature applesauce, yogurt, carrots, rice, and ice cream; do not add to any other liquids or foods; administer within 15 minutes of opening the packet; liquids may be taken subsequent to administration.
Singulair® (4 mg, 28 tablets)
